evals.name: cold architecture review
evals.prompt: A user says: review this repo's architecture. They give no intended architecture, quality goals, or scope. Describe the workflow.
evals.expected_output: The response inspects docs/manifests before asking, interviews only for missing context, builds a system map before scoring, gathers cited evidence with tool coverage, uses the scorecard, and writes a report without editing source.
assertions: The output says to inspect repository docs, ADRs, manifests, and structure before asking questions.
The output asks only for missing context that changes the architecture assessment.
The output builds a system map before scoring and explicitly forbids scoring from directory shape alone.
The output requires cited evidence and per-dimension tool coverage before findings or scores.
The output keeps the role read-only for source code and routes implementation to a mutator or engineer.

evals.name: structured question fallback
evals.prompt: A runtime has no concrete structured-question tool available. Describe how the architecture-review skill should interview the user.
evals.expected_output: The response uses a structured-question tool only when the active runtime exposes one; otherwise it asks one concise plain question with useful options and records lower confidence if the answer is ambiguous.
assertions: The output does not depend on source agent frontmatter or per-target overlays to decide tool availability.
The output uses concrete runtime tools such as AskUserQuestion or ask_user_question only when actually available.
The output falls back to one plain prose question when no structured-question tool exists.
The output records ambiguity or deferred answers as missing evidence that lowers analysis confidence.

evals.name: refactor request boundary
evals.prompt: A user asks the architect to review structural risk and then immediately refactor the code. Describe the response and handoff.
evals.expected_output: The response reviews and plans only: it produces findings and an incremental refactoring plan, refuses to edit production source itself, and hands implementation to a mutator or engineer after approval.
assertions: The output separates review, scoring, and recommendations from implementation.
The output uses architecture-plan for an incremental plan when changes are warranted.
The output refuses source edits by the architect role.
The output includes verification steps and acceptance criteria for the mutator handoff.
